text:
ByBruce Haring



ParisOlympicsorganizers apologized Sunday to anyone offended by an image of drag performers that evoked Leonardo da Vinci’s “The Last Supper” during the opening ceremony.

The scene during Friday’s ceremony featured LGBTQ+ icon, DJ and producer Barbara Butch flanked by drag artists and dancers.

Religious conservatives decried the segment, with the French Catholic Church’s conference of bishops deploring “scenes of derision” that they said made a mockery of Christianity. Complaints were also made by Russian Foreign Ministry spokesperson Maria Zakharova and the Anglican Communion in Egypt.

The ceremony’s artistic director, Thomas Jolly, claimed he meant to celebrate diversity and pay tribute to feasting and French gastronomy.Watch on Deadline

Paris 2024 spokesperson Anne Descamps responded to that during an International Olympic Committee news conference on Sunday.

“Clearly there was never an intention to show disrespect to any religious group. On the contrary, I think (with) Thomas Jolly, we really did try to celebrate community tolerance,” Descamps said. “Looking at the result of the polls that we shared, we believe that this ambition was achieved. If people have taken any offense we are, of course, really, really sorry.”

This is crazy. Opening your event by replacing Jesus and the disciples at the The Last Supper with men in drag. There are 2.4 billion Christians on earth and apparently the Olympics wanted to declare loudly to all of them, right out of the gateNOT WELCOMEpic.twitter.com/T88AmXbqXL

Get our Breaking News Alerts and Keep your inbox happy.

I will never watch the Olympics or sponser it  ever again !!!!!

If the word “If” is in an apology, IT is NOT an apology.  They fully intended to offend Christians world wide.  It was a statement; “we’re here, we’re queer, get over it” statement.  Appealing to perhaps 1 to maybe 7% of the population while offending billions of Christians.  I won’t be watching any of the Olympics this year, perhaps never again.

Absolutely disgusting and has no place in the Olympics.  It doesn’t matter any way you may look at it.  Disgusting!!!

I believe this version of the Last Supper is what the French call a “faux pas” which many Americans pronounce “fox paws” :) :) :)

It’s. Just. Theater.

Chill out, fundies.

title: Paris Olympics Organizers Apologize For Drag 'Last Supper' Depiction
author: Bruce Haring
description: Paris Olympics organizers defended their creative director, but also apologized for offending.
